elections.note: CA SoS per-county reporting-status page ('FENU'-coded row), Riverside County: 100.0% precincts reporting, Ballots Cast 189,087, Last Report stamp 'Jun 6 1:42 a.m.' (First Report 'Jun 5 8:11 p.m.') at Wayback capture 20120608222655 (2012-06-08 22:26:55 UTC). SINGLE CAPTURE at 2012-06-08 22:26:55 UTC carries this value. The next available capture (2012-06-17 19:21:15 UTC) jumps to 232,279 (Last Report 'Jun 13 3:18 p.m.'), confirming 189,087 was election night's last state before the canvass moved (non-circular bracket leg per RUNBOOK 8). NOT the pre-plateau first-tranche row (page's own early-evening captures, <99% precincts reporting, were excluded per RUNBOOK 1 and the grading discipline in scratchpad sos-status-sweep.md). Certified final 238,152 Total Voters (CA SoS Statement of Vote, Voter Participation Statistics by County, https://elections.cdn.sos.ca.gov/sov/2012-primary/pdf/03-voter-reg-stats-by-county.pdf). Arithmetic: 189,087/238,152 = 79.4%. vs_epollbook=pre, vs_asv=pre (per county_tech/adoption record: epollbook 2022, asv 2025). Grade CONFIRMED per RUNBOOK 8. Sourced from the 2026-07 CA SoS status-page sweep across the 19-county panel (scratchpad/sos-status-sweep.md, 2012-06-05 section); the cited capture and freeze/jump evidence above were independently re-derived here by matching this exact Ballots Cast figure against the raw per-capture status-page timeline (script scratchpad/build_cell_details.py), not merely transcribed from the sweep's prose summary.

Certified final = 669,627 ballots (Election Day 298,112 + VBM 371,515) from CA SoS 2012 Voter Participation Statistics by County, primary. Election-night plateau not sourceable: Riverside's Sequoia 'eresults' live page (voteinfo.net/Elections/20121106/eresults/) was not archived on/near election night, and Riverside used no Dominion ElectionSummaryReport before 2019. WebFetch blocked; curl+CDX+headless-Chrome used. Re-verified 2026-06: Riverside's /Elections/<date>/docs/ press-release archive holds only operational releases (candidate filing, VBM, early voting, certification) -- there is NO morning-after 'semi-official results' release stating an election-night total -- so SKILL route-1 (registrar semi-final release) is a dead end for this county across all years; plateau remains unsourceable. DEAD-END NOTE EXTENSION (2026-07-09, Task 9): checked whether Riverside's elections CMS (voteinfo.net / livevoterturnout.com) runs CivicPlus DocumentCenter (the module that yields guessable sequential report IDs for Nevada and Napa counties) -- it does not (CDX prefix probe of www.rivco.org/DocumentCenter: 0 captures), so no ID-walk was possible. Checked the brief's named registrar Twitter handle @RivCoVote via Wayback CDX (exact URL and matchType=prefix, unrestricted date range): zero captures under any form; a small set of plausible alternate handles (RivCoElections, VoteRiverside, RiversideVotes) also returned zero. No Facebook page candidate for Riverside's registrar was found either. No new route found; row remains as documented above. RECOVERY (2026-07-09, Task 7, NewsBank Press-Enterprise): two Nov 8, 2012 Press-Enterprise articles independently state the same countywide remaining-ballot figure. 'Buster facing defeat' (docref news/14270915258D4BE8): 'Unofficial final results from the county Registrar of Voters show Kevin Jeffries leading Buster ... with all precincts reporting. But about 183,000 vote-by-mail, provisional and damaged ballots remained to be counted.' Also states Buster 'didn't concede Wednesday, Nov. 7' (the count had not moved as of Wednesday). 'Bono Mack, Baca ousters hit clout' (docref news/14270914E8F945E8): 'With all precincts reporting ... Bono Mack said she prefers to await the final tally of 183,000 mail-in, provisional and damaged ballots that remained to be counted countywide ... The Riverside County voter registrar's office is expected to issue an updated vote count at 6 p.m. Thursday, Nov. 8.' The registrar's own posting schedule (next update explicitly Thursday 6 p.m., none between election night and the Wednesday print deadline) brackets this as the held election-night plateau (San Diego 2012 pattern: RUNBOOK 8 'county's own posting schedule bracketing the report'). PLATEAU = certified final 669,627 minus 183,000 outstanding = 486,627 counted. 486,627/669,627 = 72.67%, within the ~55-75% precinct-era calibration band (high end; Riverside's 'slow counter' reputation concerns the multi-day VBM canvass tail after this point, not the initial precincts-plus-early-VBM tranche). Not the 8 p.m. first tranche (would be far lower, before any all-precincts-reporting milestone). Comparable true. Confidence secondary: news report of a registrar-derived, not directly stated, ballots-counted figure. NEW LEG (2026-07-09, Task 7 fix pass, remedial NewsBank search): an independent, differently-bylined Nov 9 2012 Press-Enterprise article ('Many races await tallies,' docref news/14275EF93A805950, byline Jeff Horseman) confirms the promised Thursday 6 p.m. update posted and shows genuine movement forward from this baseline: 'More than 13,000 votes, which consisted of late-arriving vote-by-mail ballots as well as damaged ballots that have to be reconstructed, were added to earlier tallies. Yet to be counted are 60,000 provisional ballots ... About 86,000 vote-by-mail and 18,000 damaged ballots were left as of Thursday ... The next update is scheduled for 6 p.m. Friday.' Remaining fell from the election-night 183,000 to about 164,000 (60,000+86,000+18,000) after roughly 13,000 ballots were processed -- an independent later-document drawdown of the same remaining-ballot baseline (RUNBOOK 8's Fresno-2018 pattern), corroborating rather than replacing the San Diego 2012 posting-schedule bracket already used above. Verdict unchanged: CONFIRMED. CORRECTION (2026-07-09): the 'differently-bylined' description above is retracted: 'Many races await tallies' and 'Buster facing defeat' (the cited source_url_night article) share the same byline, Jeff Horseman. The leg's validity rests on it being a LATER document reporting a new event (the promised Thursday 6 p.m. update actually posting, with the remaining-ballot baseline drawing down), not on byline independence. Jeffries himself is quoted in that Nov 9 article: 'I'm disappointed the amount of ballots counted in the last day and a half was so small,' corroborating that the overnight movement was modest, consistent with 183,000 having been the held election-night baseline. Verdict unchanged: CONFIRMED.

Certified final = 198,102 ballots (Precinct 50,111 + VBM 147,991) from CA SoS 2014 Statewide Direct Primary Voter Participation Statistics by County, primary (filename keeps the SoS's well-known 'particpiation' misspelling). Election-night plateau not sourceable: the eresults/ directory for this date has no dated Summary_Update/SummaryN report series (unlike 2016/2018) -- only a GEMS-style frameset. Its Election Result_dtl.htm is locked to 'Last Updated: June 13, 2014 11:59 AM', Governor 847/847 precincts (100%), Total 198,102 -- exactly the SoS certified final, i.e. a fully-certified post-canvass snapshot, not a distinct election-night count. Full CDX history of that exact URL shows every capture 2014-07-01 through 2022-05-27 sharing an identical digest, i.e. frozen at the certified state since the earliest available crawl; no earlier, election-night-stage capture exists. docs/ press-release archive has no morning-after semi-official-results release (runs to the 20140613_Release_Certified.pdf certification announcement). docs.voteinfo.net does not host a dated Summary-series live for this year the way it does for 2016/2018. Plateau remains unsourceable; the 198,102 figure recovered is the certified final itself and is NOT substituted as a night count (would fabricate a false 100.00% share).

CA SoS per-county reporting-status page ('FENU'-coded row), Riverside County: 100.0% precincts reporting, Ballots Cast 265,771, Last Report stamp 'Nov 5 2:26 a.m.' (First Report 'Nov 4 8:20 p.m.') at Wayback capture 20141105141649 (2014-11-05 14:16:49 UTC). SINGLE CAPTURE at 2014-11-05 14:16:49 UTC carries this value. The next available capture (2014-11-07 08:38:01 UTC) jumps to 294,055 (Last Report 'Nov 6 6:45 p.m.'), confirming 265,771 was election night's last state before the canvass moved (non-circular bracket leg per RUNBOOK 8). NOT the pre-plateau first-tranche row (page's own early-evening captures, <99% precincts reporting, were excluded per RUNBOOK 1 and the grading discipline in scratchpad sos-status-sweep.md). Certified final 357,764 Total Voters (CA SoS Statement of Vote, Voter Participation Statistics by County, https://elections.cdn.sos.ca.gov/sov/2014-general/pdf/03-voter-particpiation-stats-by-county.pdf). Arithmetic: 265,771/357,764 = 74.29%. vs_epollbook=pre, vs_asv=pre (per county_tech/adoption record: epollbook 2022, asv 2025). Grade CONFIRMED per RUNBOOK 8. Sourced from the 2026-07 CA SoS status-page sweep across the 19-county panel (scratchpad/sos-status-sweep.md, 2014-11-04 section); the cited capture and freeze/jump evidence above were independently re-derived here by matching this exact Ballots Cast figure against the raw per-capture status-page timeline (script scratchpad/build_cell_details.py), not merely transcribed from the sweep's prose summary. DEAD-END NOTE EXTENSION (2026-07-09, Task 9): checked whether Riverside's elections CMS (voteinfo.net / livevoterturnout.com) runs CivicPlus DocumentCenter (the module that yields guessable sequential report IDs for Nevada and Napa counties) -- it does not (CDX prefix probe of www.rivco.org/DocumentCenter: 0 captures), so no ID-walk was possible. Checked the brief's named registrar Twitter handle @RivCoVote via Wayback CDX (exact URL and matchType=prefix, unrestricted date range): zero captures under any form; a small set of plausible alternate handles (RivCoElections, VoteRiverside, RiversideVotes) also returned zero. No Facebook page candidate for Riverside's registrar was found either. No new route found; row remains as documented above. RECOVERY (2026-07-09, Task 7, NewsBank Press-Enterprise): 'Council members succeed, struggle' (Nov 6 2014, docref news/151717B89F05D948): 'The Riverside County Registrar of Voters office announced that 93,000 votes are still to be processed, including 66,000 vote-by-mail ballots, 19,000 provisional ballots and 8,000 damaged ballots. The next update will be posted at 6 p.m. tonight' (same Riverside pattern as 2012: held since election night, first post-night update two days later). Independent cross-check, 'a new low in area's Voter turnout?' (same date, docref news/151717B8F7250120): 'In Riverside County, turnout was 29.8 percent as of Wednesday. It likely will end up at about 39 or 40 percent once all the ballots are counted, said Registrar Rebecca Spencer' -- Spencer's '39 or 40 percent' estimate matches the eventual certified 40.13% almost exactly, validating the article; 29.8% x 891,575 registered = 265,689, within 0.3% of the 93,000-derived 264,764 (independent method, second bylined article). PLATEAU = 357,764 minus 93,000 outstanding = 264,764. 264,764/357,764 = 74.01%, high end of the ~55-75% precinct-era band but corroborated two independent ways. Comparable true. Confidence secondary (news-derived registrar figure). NEW LEG (2026-07-09, Task 7 fix pass, remedial NewsBank search): two independent, differently-bylined follow-ups confirm the promised 'update ... at 6 p.m. tonight' posted and show the remaining-ballot baseline drawing down from election night's 93,000: 'Chabot concedes to Aguilar in House race' (docref news/15176B29AEBF4130, Jeff Horseman, Nov 7 2014): 'A vote-count update posted Thursday afternoon showed Aguilar ... leading Chabot ... by 2,291 votes ... Aguilar's lead was roughly 2,000 votes as of early Wednesday morning' (confirms the Thursday update posted, with movement since Wednesday). 'Lead wanes; results unchanged' (docref news/151813F1AA650318, Imran Ghori, Nov 9 2014): 'Jackson's lead has slimmed since Tuesday night ... and Thursday, when it fell to 22 votes as the Riverside County Registrar of Voters continued sorting damaged, mail-in and provisional ballots ... The Riverside County Registrar of Voters will issue another update Wednesday as it counts about 24,000 remaining mail-in, provisional and damaged ballots' (remaining down from 93,000 on election night to 24,000 by the following Sunday, a continuous drawdown of the same baseline, RUNBOOK 8's Fresno-2018 pattern). Verdict unchanged: CONFIRMED. CORRECTION (2026-07-09): the Aguilar/Chabot sentence above ('A vote-count update posted Thursday afternoon showed Aguilar ... leading Chabot ... by 2,291 votes') is WITHDRAWN as Riverside evidence: the 31st Congressional District (Aguilar v. Chabot) lies entirely within San Bernardino County, so that Thursday-afternoon update is San Bernardino's registrar's posting, not Riverside's. The SAME article ('Chabot concedes to Aguilar in House race', docref news/15176B29AEBF4130) does carry Riverside-relevant evidence, about the Stone/Garcia state Senate race: 'Riverside County Supervisor Jeff Stone secured victory in the race for a state Senate seat. Stone now leads by 8,405 votes over fellow Republican Bonnie Garcia. He had a roughly 8,000-vote lead going into Thursday evening.' That is the article's Riverside-relevant leg, not the withdrawn Aguilar/Chabot sentence. The verdict is carried regardless by the Ghori 'Lead wanes; results unchanged' leg (Moreno Valley council lead 68 votes Tuesday night -> 22 Thursday -> 18 late Friday; countywide remaining falling from election night's 93,000 to about 24,000 by the following Sunday), independent of the withdrawn quote. Verdict unchanged: CONFIRMED. SUPERSEDED (2026-07-10 merge): the 2026-07-09 recovery above landed 264,764 ballots (74.01%), but this row carries 265,771 (74.29%) from the 2026-07 independent-source adjudication documented earlier in this note; the newer bracket/freeze-confirmed plateau evidence wins on value and flags, and the recovery trail is retained per RUNBOOK 5.4.

Election-night plateau = 249,970 ballots ('Total Ballots Cast' countywide, Riverside County column) from the official Riverside Sequoia 'Official Semi-Final Results' report Summary_Update8.pdf, Run Date/Time 6/8/16 3:18:26 AM (the last-of-night report; posting cadence ~hourly from 8:02 PM through 3:18 AM, matching the same hourly-until-~3am cadence documented elsewhere for this county). The next available report in the series, Summary_Update9.pdf, is Run Date/Time 6/10/16 4:49:49 PM -- two days later, proving the canvass had resumed and Update8 was the true election-night plateau (runbook 8 leg: report series' next file being days later). Update17/Summary_FINAL_0705.pdf both show 403,828, exactly the SoS certified total, confirming the series runs night-through-certification. NOT the 8:02 PM first tranche (Update1, 157,867 -- 39.1% of final, the classic mistake). 249,970/403,828 = 61.90%. Certified final 403,828 (Precinct 127,086 + VBM 276,742) from CA SoS 2016 Presidential Primary Voter Participation Statistics by County, primary. Series enumerated via CDX (voteinfo.net/Elections/20160607/eresults/, matchType=prefix); PDF fetched via Wayback raw id_ replay and content-verified 2026-07-10 (Total Ballots Cast 249,970, Run Date/Time 6/8/16 3:18:26 AM). Pre-epollbook (2022 VCA rollout), pre-ASV (2025).

CA SoS per-county reporting-status page ('SF'-coded row), Riverside County: 100.0% precincts reporting, Ballots Cast 481,315, Last Report stamp 'Nov 9 5:52 a.m.' (First Report 'Nov 8 8:31 p.m.') at Wayback capture 20161110185817 (2016-11-10 18:58:17 UTC). Arithmetic: 481,315/769,193 = 62.57%. vs_epollbook=pre, vs_asv=pre (per county_tech/adoption record: epollbook 2022, asv 2025). Grade CONFIRMED per RUNBOOK 8. Sourced from the 2026-07 CA SoS status-page sweep across the 19-county panel (scratchpad/sos-status-sweep.md, 2016-11-08 section); the cited capture and freeze/jump evidence above were independently re-derived here by matching this exact Ballots Cast figure against the raw per-capture status-page timeline (script scratchpad/build_cell_details.py), not merely transcribed from the sweep's prose summary. DEAD-END NOTE EXTENSION (2026-07-09, Task 9): checked whether Riverside's elections CMS (voteinfo.net / livevoterturnout.com) runs CivicPlus DocumentCenter (the module that yields guessable sequential report IDs for Nevada and Napa counties) -- it does not (CDX prefix probe of www.rivco.org/DocumentCenter: 0 captures), so no ID-walk was possible. Checked the brief's named registrar Twitter handle @RivCoVote via Wayback CDX (exact URL and matchType=prefix, unrestricted date range): zero captures under any form; a small set of plausible alternate handles (RivCoElections, VoteRiverside, RiversideVotes) also returned zero. No Facebook page candidate for Riverside's registrar was found either. No new route found; row remains as documented above. RECOVERY (2026-07-09, Task 7, NewsBank Press-Enterprise): two Nov 10 2016 local-race articles carry identical registrar boilerplate -- 'One Eastvale council race especially close' (docref news/16095824826D46E0) and 'Corona voters back council districts, pick new treasurer' (docref news/160958246D11FAA8): 'The figures, posted by the Riverside County Registrar of Voters Office early Wednesday, are not final. Officials have yet to count 262,000 provisional, damaged and vote-by-mail ballots submitted countywide.' A third, differently-bylined Nov 10 article ('Familiar faces on southwest councils', docref news/16095823F5443EE8) corroborates ('more than 200,000 ballots need to be counted') and states 'The next update from the registrar's office is set for 6 p.m. Friday' -- bracketing the Wednesday-posted count as unchanged through Thursday (RUNBOOK 8 posting-schedule leg). The subsequent Friday update ('Latest vote count: No big changes', Nov 12, docref news/160A0028EF5A1DB8) shows 136,000+67,000+9,000=212,000 remaining, a lower later-canvass figure consistent in direction/magnitude with 262,000 minus ~2 days of processing (confirms 262,000 was the earlier held state, not itself post-canvass). PLATEAU = 769,193 minus 262,000 outstanding = 507,193. 507,193/769,193 = 65.94%, closely matching SF's own 2016 calibration (66%). Comparable true. Confidence secondary (news-derived registrar figure). SUPERSEDED (2026-07-10 merge): the 2026-07-09 recovery above landed 507,193 ballots (65.94%), but this row carries 481,315 (62.57%) from the 2026-07 independent-source adjudication documented earlier in this note; the newer bracket/freeze-confirmed plateau evidence wins on value and flags, and the recovery trail is retained per RUNBOOK 5.4.

Election-night plateau = 193,152 ballots ('Total Ballots Cast' countywide, Riverside County column) from the official Riverside Sequoia 'Official Semi-Final Results' report Summary8.pdf, Run Date/Time 6/6/18 6:25:10 AM. Wayback's own crawl of the eresults/ directory only preserved Summary12.pdf (already mid-canvass, Run Date/Time 6/19/18 4:32:44 PM, 335,242 ballots); the full Summary1-13 series was instead recovered from docs.voteinfo.net, a still-live document CDN that continues to serve Riverside's historical dated-report PDFs years after the front-end site (www.voteinfo.net) was redesigned and its own copies 404'd (Summary14+ 404 there too). Report cadence: 8:00 PM through 6:25 AM roughly hourly (Summary1-8), then Summary9 jumps to 6/8/18 5:22:39 PM -- a 2-day gap proving Summary8 was the last report of the continuous election-night sequence (runbook 8 leg: report series' next file being days later). NOT the 8:00 PM first tranche (Summary1, 119,452 -- 34.5% of final). 193,152/346,472 = 55.75%. Certified final 346,472 (Precinct 91,667 + VBM 254,805) from CA SoS 2018 Statewide Direct Primary Voter Participation Statistics by County, primary; independently corroborated by the eresults GEMS frameset (Election Result_dtl.htm), frozen at 'Last Updated: June 29, 2018', showing the identical 346,472. Since docs.voteinfo.net is a live (mutable) source, Summary8.pdf was archived to Wayback 2026-07-10 (web.archive.org/save/, snapshot 20260710205406) for a stable citation; content-verified 2026-07-10 (Total Ballots Cast 193,152, Run Date/Time 6/6/18 6:25:10 AM). Pre-epollbook, pre-ASV.

Certified final = 650,545 ballots (Election Day 194,606 + VBM 455,939) from CA SoS 2018 Voter Participation Statistics by County; corroborated by Riverside Summary_Final.pdf (Run Date/Time 12/7/18). Primary. Election-night plateau not sourceable: earliest Wayback capture of the eresults page is 2018-11-28 (post-certification); no election-night-stage snapshot exists. Re-verified 2026-06: Riverside's /Elections/20181106/docs/ press-release archive runs candidate-filing -> VBM -> early-voting -> 20181105_Release_Encouraged and 20181106_QualWriteInResults, then certification -- NO morning-after 'semi-official results' release stating an election-night total; plateau remains unsourceable. DEAD-END NOTE EXTENSION (2026-07-09, Task 9): checked whether Riverside's elections CMS (voteinfo.net / livevoterturnout.com) runs CivicPlus DocumentCenter (the module that yields guessable sequential report IDs for Nevada and Napa counties) -- it does not (CDX prefix probe of www.rivco.org/DocumentCenter: 0 captures), so no ID-walk was possible. Checked the brief's named registrar Twitter handle @RivCoVote via Wayback CDX (exact URL and matchType=prefix, unrestricted date range): zero captures under any form; a small set of plausible alternate handles (RivCoElections, VoteRiverside, RiversideVotes) also returned zero. No Facebook page candidate for Riverside's registrar was found either. No new route found; row remains as documented above. RECOVERY (2026-07-09, Task 7, NewsBank Press-Enterprise): 'Elections - Many Inland ballots still not processed or counted' (docref news/16F9051D0AED5530): 'Final unofficial vote counts aren't expected to be released until 6 p.m. today for Riverside County ... Riverside County Registrar of Voters Rebecca Spencer said she had a crew of about 300 workers processing ballots overnight Tuesday. According to Wednesday vote totals, they counted about 300,000 ballots. On Wednesday morning, Spencer's staff started going over about 200,000 vote-by-mail, 45,000 provisional and 5,000 damaged ballots.' Spencer is directly quoted the same Wednesday: 'We're not counting anything today ... We are sorting out all the materials from the polling places' -- confirming the 300,000 figure was frozen (no counting that day) at the time of the report, a self-confirming plateau. PLATEAU = 300,000 (directly stated, not a subtraction derivation). 300,000/650,545 = 46.12%. Below the general ~55-75% precinct-era band but explicitly within the brief's 'mid-40s-to-60s on-night shares are plausible' carve-out for Riverside's notoriously slow 2010s counting (this election also saw unusually high turnout and long lines per the same article, plausibly slowing the count further). Comparable true. Confidence secondary (news-quoted registrar figure, round number). NEW LEG (2026-07-09, Task 7 fix pass, remedial NewsBank search): an independent, differently-bylined Nov 9 2018 Press-Enterprise article ('Politics - Essayli retakes lead over Cervantes,' docref news/16F95A20B5E20588, Jeff Horseman) reports the Thursday-evening state: 'About 188,000 vote-by-mail, 45,000 provisional and 5,000 damaged ballots remained to be counted as of Thursday evening.' Compared to the Wednesday-morning breakdown above (200,000 VBM + 45,000 provisional + 5,000 damaged = 250,000 remaining), VBM fell only from 200,000 to 188,000 -- about 12,000 ballots processed across all of Wednesday-into-Thursday-evening, consistent with (not contradicting) Spencer's 'not counting anything today' Wednesday freeze, and a genuine later-document drawdown of the same remaining-ballot baseline (RUNBOOK 8's Fresno-2018 pattern). Verdict unchanged: CONFIRMED. RECONCILIATION (2026-07-09): 300,000 counted overnight Tuesday plus the article's itemized ~250,000 remaining (200,000 VBM + 45,000 provisional + 5,000 damaged) sums to ~550,000, versus the certified final 650,545 -- an undisclosed ~100,545-ballot gap. This is plausibly late-arriving VBM and provisionals not yet itemized as of the Wednesday report (the source article does not address it); stated here as interpretation, not sourced. ROUND-NUMBER CAVEAT (2026-07-09): 46.12% is a 2-decimal percent derived from an 'about 300,000' round figure; a few-thousand-ballot wobble in the true count is expected and would not materially change the percent.

Election-night plateau = 191,996 ballots ('Voters Cast: 191,996 of 1,304,447') from the official Riverside Dominion EMS 'Semi-Official Election Results' report ElectionSummaryReportRPT6.pdf, timestamp 6/8/2022 12:31:41 AM -- the first report to reach 795/795 (100.00%) precincts reported, i.e. election-day in-person voting fully tallied. Wayback only preserved RPT6 (mid-canvass 8/18/2022 crawl) and RPT14 among the numbered series; the full RPT1-16 run was recovered from docs.voteinfo.net (the same live document CDN that also rescued the 2018 primary's Summary series above; RPT17+ 404 there). Report cadence: 8:03 PM through 12:31 AM roughly hourly (RPT1-6), then RPT7 jumps to 6/8/2022 4:52:51 PM (next afternoon, 16+ hour gap) and every subsequent report is a once-daily canvass update through RPT16 (6/30/2022 = 375,610, exactly the SoS certified final). NOT the 8:03 PM first tranche (RPT1, 155,963 -- 41.5% of final). 191,996/375,610 = 51.12%. Certified final 375,610 (Precinct 23,640 + VBM 351,970) from CA SoS 2022 Primary Voter Participation Statistics by County, primary. RPT6.pdf archived to Wayback 2026-07-10 (web.archive.org/save/, snapshot 20260710205902); content-verified 2026-07-10 (Voters Cast 191,996 of 1,304,447, Precincts Reported 795 of 795). ROLLOUT-TIMING FINDING: per the county-tech record (data/research/county-tech/riverside-ca.json, epollbook, primary confidence, citing the Registrar's Election Administration Plan), the Board of Supervisors approved VCA implementation 2021-12-14 and this June 7, 2022 primary is explicitly named the FIRST election under the new model (vote centers + direct-EMS-connection digital roster replacing paper rosters at polling places) -- vs_epollbook is therefore 'post' for THIS row, not merely for elections after it; every row through 2018 is cleanly 'pre'. CONFOUND: this is also the first all-mail VCA election, so any 2018-vs-2022 election-night-share comparison conflates the e-pollbook change with the simultaneous VCA/all-mail-ballot shift; recorded, not adjusted for. vs_asv: pre (ASV adopted 2025 per county-tech record).

Election-night plateau = 205,813 ballots ('Voters Cast: 205,813 of 1,310,928 (15.70%)'; Governor Times Cast 205,813) from the official Riverside Dominion EMS 'Semi-Official Election Results' report, Last-Modified 11/09/2022 02:04 PST (the ~2 a.m. final election-night report, within the hourly-until-3am window). This held as the live page until the first canvass update (300,498, Last-Modified 11/11/2022) -- i.e. it plateaued through the next day before the multi-day canvass resumed, matching the metric. Corroborated by Idyllwild Town Crier (Nov 9, 2022): election-night counting complete at ~205,000 with ~335,000 ballots remaining. 205,813/604,617 = 34.0%. Certified final 604,617 (Election Day 59,812 + VBM 544,805) from CA SoS, primary. snapshot 20221109110809 used. First epollbook general.

CA SoS per-county reporting-status page ('U'-coded row), Riverside County: 100.0% precincts reporting, Ballots Cast 213,998, Last Report stamp 'Mar 6 3:01 a.m.' (First Report 'Mar 5 8:30 p.m.') at Wayback capture 20240306144409 (2024-03-06 14:44:09 UTC). FROZEN: the identical Ballots Cast figure 213,998 recurs across 2 captures, 2024-03-06 14:44:09 UTC through 2024-03-06 18:05:49 UTC, before the next available capture (2024-03-07 03:52:36 UTC) jumps to 228,760 (Last Report 'Mar 6 6:20 p.m.'), marking the canvass resuming. NOT the pre-plateau first-tranche row (page's own early-evening captures, <99% precincts reporting, were excluded per RUNBOOK 1 and the grading discipline in scratchpad sos-status-sweep.md). Certified final 409,269 Total Voters (CA SoS Statement of Vote, Voter Participation Statistics by County, https://elections.cdn.sos.ca.gov/sov/2024-primary/sov/03-voter-participation-stats-by-county.pdf). Arithmetic: 213,998/409,269 = 52.29%. vs_epollbook=post, vs_asv=pre (per county_tech/adoption record: epollbook 2022, asv 2025). Grade CONFIRMED per RUNBOOK 8. Sourced from the 2026-07 CA SoS status-page sweep across the 19-county panel (scratchpad/sos-status-sweep.md, 2024-03-05 section); the cited capture and freeze/jump evidence above were independently re-derived here by matching this exact Ballots Cast figure against the raw per-capture status-page timeline (script scratchpad/build_cell_details.py), not merely transcribed from the sweep's prose summary.

611,101 'Ballots Counted' from the earliest Wayback capture of Riverside's livevoterturnout ENR, embedded timestamp 'Updated: 11/6/2024 5:35:21 PM' -- the FIRST daily canvass update (afternoon of the day after the Nov 5 election), NOT the ~3 a.m. election-night plateau. Riverside reports hourly until ~3 a.m. (confirmed by the registrar's own 'November 5 Election Night Results Reporting' release, rivco.org, dated Nov 4, 2024: 'results will continue to be uploaded every hour until approximately 3 a.m.') then updates daily (excl. Sundays). 611,101/959,098 = 63.7% slightly OVER-states the election-night share and is NOT strictly stage-comparable to the clean 2 a.m. 2022 figure. Next captures: 685,126 (11/9), 912,011 (11/15). Certified final 959,098 (Election Day 170,437 + VBM 788,661) from CA SoS, primary. snapshot 20241107034053 used. Post-epollbook, pre-ASV. Re-verified 2026-06: the only Wayback ENR captures are 20241107034053 (the 11/6 17:35 update shown above) and 20241109 -- there is NO capture in the poll-close-to-3 a.m. window; Riverside posted no 2024 Dominion EMS ElectionSummaryReport to voteinfo.net/Elections/20241105/docs/ (none archived), issued no morning-after 'semi-official results' release stating an election-night total, and local news (Riverside Record, Raincross Gazette) state precincts-reporting but no countywide election-night ballot count. The ~3 a.m. plateau is therefore genuinely unarchived; 611,101 is retained as the nearest datapoint (an upper bound) and flagged not-comparable per comparable:false above. CORRECTION (2026-07-10): landed value replaced with the CA SoS county-reporting-status page's bracket-confirmed overnight plateau, 547,742 ballots, Last Report stamp 'Nov 6 5:02 a.m.' The SoS timeline shows Riverside hit 100% precincts (1,345/1,345) at capture 20241106073132 (466,214), then climbed 481,400 (12:05 AM) -> 496,024 (12:59 AM) -> 510,623 (2:05 AM) -> 547,742 (Last Report 5:02 AM), which then held FROZEN byte-identical across 3 consecutive captures (20241106133254, 20241106154527, 20241106171813, ~6:32 AM to ~5:18 PM Nov 6) before the next capture (20241107015446, Last Report 'Nov 6 5:34 p.m.') jumped to the SAME 611,101 figure recorded above as the next-day-afternoon canvass update. 547,742 is thus a genuine ~5 a.m. overnight plateau (12+ hours stable), stage-comparable to the 2022 general's clean ~2 a.m. figure; comparable:false and the flag are removed and this row is now a usable pre/post datapoint. New pct = 547,742/959,098 = 57.11%. source_url_night moved to the SoS status-page capture (Wayback 20241106133254 of electionresults.sos.ca.gov/returns/status); confidence -> primary. The 611,101 next-day ceiling is retained above as history.
